Acer buergerianum.bark: Grey-brown, exfoliating in long strips on older trees
Acer buergerianum.fruit: Samara, wings parallel to slightly divergent

Acer rubrum.bark: Smooth grey when young, shaggy/darker with age

Betula nigra.bark: Salmon-pink to reddish-brown, extremely papery and peeling

Carpinus caroliniana.fruit: Small nutlet at the base of a 3-lobed bract

Cercidiphyllum japonicum.bark: Brownish-grey, shaggy with age; fallen leaves smell like burnt sugar

Lagerstroemia indica.bark: Extremely smooth, exfoliating to reveal pinkish-inner bark
Lagerstroemia indica.flower: Showy terminal panicles (white, pink, red, lavender)

Liquidambar styraciflua.bark: Greyish-brown, deeply furrowed, often with corky ridges on twigs

Liriodendron tulipifera.bark: Grey, furrowed into long, rough, interlacing ridges

Quercus alba.leafMargin: Pinnately lobed (7-9 lobes), lobes are rounded
Quercus alba.bark: Light grey, shaggy/peeling in vertical strips

Quercus phellos.bark: Grey-black, becoming deeply furrowed and ridged

Cornus florida.leafMargin: Entire, veins curve toward leaf tip (arcuate)
Cornus florida.flower: Small green cluster surrounded by 4 large white/pink bracts

Cercis canadensis.bark: Dark grey-brown, becoming scaly with age
Cercis canadensis.flower: Small magenta-pink pea-like flowers along branches

Ginkgo biloba.leafArrangement: Alternate, often in clusters on short spurs
Ginkgo biloba.fruit: Plum-like (naked seed) with foul-smelling fleshy coat

Magnolia grandiflora.bark: Grey, smooth when young, scaly when old
